# i66.org

> A self-hosted library of 343 plain-text shell commands for cloud and Linux infrastructure. Each command is a single bash one-liner served over HTTP and executed locally via the i66 CLI or curl.

## What it is

i66.org is an open-source command server. Every path on the site (e.g. `http://i66.org/aws/ec2/list`) returns a plain bash command as `text/plain`. You fetch and run it — no installation required beyond the i66 CLI or curl.

Commands use environment variables for targets: `DOMAIN=`, `HOST=`, `IP=`, `URL=`, `RELEASE=`, `DB=`, `PORT=`, `ZONE_ID=`, `DIST_ID=`, `REPO=`, `RESOURCE_ID=`, `QUEUE_URL=`, `PATCH_GROUP=`, `INSTANCE=`, `TABLE=`.

## Usage

```bash
# Install the CLI
go build -o i66 i66.org.go && ./i66 <command>

# Or use curl directly (no install)
bash <(curl -s http://i66.org/sys/disk)
curl -s http://i66.org/aws/ec2/list | bash

# With environment variable targets
DOMAIN=example.com i66 net/dig/a
HOST=8.8.8.8       i66 net/ping
DB=myapp           i66 db/pg/tables
RELEASE=myapp      i66 helm/values
```

Commands require Linux or a Linux-compatible shell (bash, systemctl, ss, ip, etc.). On Windows use WSL2.

## Command categories

### AWS (90 commands)
Subpaths: `aws/ec2/`, `aws/vpc/`, `aws/eks/`, `aws/s3/`, `aws/iam/`, `aws/rds/`, `aws/lambda/`, `aws/ecs/`, `aws/cfn/`, `aws/sqs/`, `aws/sns/`, `aws/ecr/`, `aws/ssm/`, `aws/secrets/`, `aws/dynamo/`, `aws/cloudfront/`, `aws/apigw/`, `aws/r53/`, `aws/cost/`, `aws/cw/`

Examples:
- `aws/ec2/list` — All instances with ID, name, type, state, IPs
- `aws/vpc/subnets` — All subnets across VPCs
- `aws/eks/clusters` — EKS clusters and Kubernetes versions
- `aws/iam/password-policy` — Account password policy
- `aws/sqs/depth` — Messages visible per queue (QUEUE_URL=)
- `aws/ecr/scan-findings` — Critical/High CVEs (REPO=)
- `aws/dynamo/indexes` — Global Secondary Indexes per table
- `aws/cloudfront/invalidations` — Recent invalidations (DIST_ID=)
- `aws/ssm/patch-compliance` — Instance patch compliance (PATCH_GROUP=)

### Azure (36 commands)
Subpaths: `azure/vm/`, `azure/rg/`, `azure/aks/`, `azure/storage/`, `azure/network/`, `azure/db/`, `azure/monitor/`, `azure/app/`, `azure/security/`, `azure/cost/`

Examples:
- `azure/network/nsgs` — Network Security Groups
- `azure/db/cosmos` — Cosmos DB accounts
- `azure/monitor/action-groups` — Action groups
- `azure/security/recommendations` — Defender for Cloud recommendations
- `azure/app/acr` — Azure Container Registry instances

### GCP (38 commands)
Subpaths: `gcp/vm/`, `gcp/sql/`, `gcp/gke/`, `gcp/iam/`, `gcp/network/`, `gcp/run/`, `gcp/functions/`, `gcp/pubsub/`, `gcp/monitor/`

Examples:
- `gcp/network/firewall-rules` — Firewall rules
- `gcp/run/domains` — Domain mappings
- `gcp/monitor/incidents` — Active incidents
- `gcp/functions/logs` — Recent function execution logs
- `gcp/iam/service-accounts` — Service accounts in the project

### System — 30 commands (`sys/`)
Disk, memory, CPU, processes, ports, security posture, storage, I/O, logs, audit.

Examples: `sys/disk`, `sys/top10cpu`, `sys/suid`, `sys/world-writable`, `sys/iostat`, `sys/auth-log`, `sys/reboots`

### Docker — 12 commands (`docker/`)
Examples: `docker/ps`, `docker/stats`, `docker/dangling-images`, `docker/build-cache`, `docker/compose-ps`

### Kubernetes — 21 commands (`k8s/`)
Examples: `k8s/pods-failed`, `k8s/hpa`, `k8s/network-policies`, `k8s/crds`, `k8s/quotas`

### Network / DNS — 24 commands (`net/`, `ssl/`)
DNS lookups (A, AAAA, MX, NS, TXT, CNAME, PTR, SOA, CAA, SRV), HTTP inspection, port checks, TLS versions, certificate chain, ciphers, HSTS, SANs, CT logs.

Examples: `net/dig/aaaa`, `net/port-check`, `net/redirects`, `ssl/ciphers`, `ssl/ct`

### Git — 15 commands (`git/`)
Examples: `git/log`, `git/ahead-behind`, `git/large-files`, `git/contributors`, `git/unmerged`

### Helm — 12 commands (`helm/`)
Examples: `helm/releases`, `helm/outdated`, `helm/history` (RELEASE=), `helm/manifests` (RELEASE=)

### Terraform — 10 commands (`terraform/`)
Examples: `terraform/state`, `terraform/drift`, `terraform/output`, `terraform/providers`

### Databases — 20 commands (`db/pg/`, `db/mysql/`)
PostgreSQL: connections, slow queries, locks, bloat, replication, vacuum status.
MySQL: processlist, InnoDB locks, replication lag, variables.

Examples: `db/pg/slow-queries`, `db/pg/bloat`, `db/mysql/locks` (DB=)

### Redis — 10 commands (`redis/`)
Examples: `redis/memory`, `redis/slowlog`, `redis/keyspace`, `redis/latency`

### nginx — 8 commands (`nginx/`)
Examples: `nginx/status`, `nginx/access-top`, `nginx/status-codes`, `nginx/bandwidth`

### HashiCorp Vault — 10 commands (`vault/`)
Examples: `vault/status`, `vault/secrets`, `vault/leases`, `vault/namespaces`

## Source

- GitHub: https://github.com/ivandir/i66.org
- Releases (pre-built binaries): https://github.com/ivandir/i66.org/releases
- License: MIT
